content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as hierarchical curiosity filters, where long-term memory pruning only retains experiences that triggered intrinsic curiosity signals (from ICM/RND frameworks) rather than just task-relevant information. This creates a self-reinforcing memory loop where more curious exploration over time expands the agent's active learning query coverage beyond narrow task distributions.
